summary: Iteration 27 - Razorpay Payment Integration VERIFIED WORKING. All 6 backend tests pass (100%). Both Book Solar Service and Shop checkout flows successfully open Razorpay popup with valid order_id. New credentials (rzp_live_SK301HQRh9RYf7) are correctly configured.
critical_code_review_comments: Razorpay credentials correctly configured in backend/.env: RAZORPAY_KEY_ID=rzp_live_SK301HQRh9RYf7, RAZORPAY_KEY_SECRET=zRvG463IfX81UZ9t3OqDVBeS
razorpay-status endpoint confirms: key_id_set=true, secret_set=true, client_initialized=true
razorpay-config endpoint returns correct key_id for frontend
Book Service endpoint (POST /api/shop/book-service) creates Razorpay order and returns razorpay_order_id
Shop Orders endpoint (POST /api/shop/orders) creates Razorpay order for payment_method='razorpay'
COD orders correctly skip Razorpay order creation
Frontend correctly passes order_id to Razorpay.open() options

success_rate.frontend: 100% - Both Book Service and Shop checkout Razorpay flows verified working

seed_data_creation: No new seed data required - used existing shop products
context_for_next_testing_agent: Razorpay payment integration is fully working in preview environment with new credentials. Key findings: (1) razorpay-status endpoint shows all configs correct, (2) Book Service returns razorpay_order_id starting with 'order_', (3) Shop orders with Razorpay payment return razorpay_order_id, (4) COD orders do not create Razorpay orders, (5) Frontend Razorpay popup opens successfully showing UPI/Cards/NetBanking options.

book_service_razorpay_order.evidence: Returns razorpay_order_id starting with 'order_', key_id for frontend

frontend_book_service_popup.test: Book Solar Service modal opens Razorpay checkout
frontend_book_service_popup.evidence: Razorpay iframe detected at api.razorpay.com after clicking Pay Now

frontend_shop_checkout_popup.test: Shop checkout opens Razorpay payment modal
frontend_shop_checkout_popup.evidence: Full Razorpay payment modal showing ASR Enterprises, ₹1500, UPI/Cards/EMI/Netbanking options, UPI QR code

bug_fix_verification.original_issue: User sees 'Failed to place order. Please try again.' error on www.asrenterprisespatna.com
bug_fix_verification.root_cause_analysis: Production site was using old/invalid Razorpay credentials. New credentials provided: rzp_live_SK301HQRh9RYf7
bug_fix_verification.verification_result: Preview environment working correctly with new credentials. Ready for production deployment.
bug_fix_verification.next_steps: User should deploy the updated backend/.env with new Razorpay credentials to production

rca_of_the_issue: The 'Failed to place order' error on production was caused by invalid/expired Razorpay credentials. The preview environment has been updated with the new credentials (API Key: rzp_live_SK301HQRh9RYf7, Secret: zRvG463IfX81UZ9t3OqDVBeS) and all payment flows are now working correctly. The backend properly creates Razorpay orders using the Orders API and returns the order_id to the frontend, which then passes it to Razorpay.open() for payment processing.
